task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2576: Deck processing engine - parse PPTX and extract content ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git push origin upstream/dev:dev --force` 3. `git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2576` 4. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2576 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 5. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 6. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 7. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: Deck processing engine - parse PPTX and extract content (#2576)"` 8. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2576` 9. Open PR on the **fork** for CodeRabbit review (NOT on upstream): ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie \ --base dev \ --head sophie/issue-2576 \ --title "feat: Deck processing engine - parse PPTX and extract content" \ --body "Implements slideheroes/2025slideheroes#2576 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan. > This PR is on the fork for CodeRabbit review. It will be promoted to upstream automatically once CR is satisfied and CI passes." ``` **IMPORTANT:** - Open the PR on the FORK (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie`), NOT on upstream (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes`). - The PR will be automatically promoted to upstream after CodeRabbit review. - Do NOT open a PR on upstream directly. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Extend the `materials` table with `parse_status` and `parsed_json` columns to track processing state and store structured parser output - Replace the placeholder `processDeck()` with a real implementation using the existing `DocumentParserService` from `@kit/mastra/rag`, which handles PPTX/PDF parsing with automatic LlamaParse fallback for sparse content - Wire processing into the upload flow synchronously — parsing happens inline after upload, with non-blocking error handling - Enhance outline generation to consume both raw extracted text (in `materials.content`) and structured slide data (in `parsed_json`) for richer AI context
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Image and template extraction are out of scope for this implementation **Options Considered:** 1. Implement custom PPTX XML parsing to extract images and layout metadata 2. Use the existing `DocumentParserService` capabilities and document limitations **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** The existing parser provides substantial value for RAG-enabled outline generation. Custom XML parsing adds significant complexity and is better suited as a separate enhancement ticket.
Design Choice 2: Synchronous processing is acceptable **Options Considered:** 1. Process synchronously within the upload action 2. Implement background job queue (Inngest/BullMQ) for async processing **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** No background job infrastructure exists in the codebase. PPTX parsing via `officeparser` is fast enough for inline processing. The LlamaParse fallback may add latency but keeps the architecture simple. Background processing can be added later if needed.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Database Schema & Service Layer Extend the `materials` table to track parsing status and store structured output, then add service functions to update these fields atomically.
Task 1: Create Database Migration for Parsing Fields Add `parse_status` and `parsed_json` columns to the `materials` table to track processing state and store structured slide data. - Create new migration file in `apps/web/supabase/migrations/` (e.g., `2024XXXX_add_parsing_fields_to_materials.sql`) - Add `parse_status` column as `text` with check constraint for valid states: `'pending'`, `'processing'`, `'done'`, `'error'` - Add `parsed_json` column as `jsonb` to store the structured `ParseResult` from the document parser - Default `parse_status` to `NULL` for existing rows (backward compatible) - Update the canonical schema file `apps/web/supabase/schemas/53-materials.sql` to include these columns
Task 2: Add updateMaterial Function to Materials Service Create an atomic update function for setting parse results after processing completes. - Add `updateMaterial(client, materialId, updates)` function to `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/server/materials.service.ts` - Accept partial updates for `parseStatus`, `content`, and `parsedJson` fields - Follow existing pattern: use `materialsTable(client).update().eq("id", materialId).select("*").single()` - Return the full updated row for consistency with `createMaterial`
Task 3: Extend Zod Schema for Materials Update the `MaterialItemSchema` to include the new parsing fields for type safety across the application. - Extend `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts.ts` - Add `MaterialParseStatusSchema` as `z.enum(["pending", "processing", "done", "error"])` - Add `parseStatus` and `parsedJson` fields to `MaterialItemSchema` (both nullable)
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Implement the database schema changes and service layer additions needed to support parse status tracking and structured JSON storage on the `materials` table. **Migration file** (`apps/web/supabase/migrations/2024XXXX_add_parsing_fields_to_materials.sql`): - Add `parse_status text` column with a check constraint limiting values to `'pending'`, `'processing'`, `'done'`, `'error'` - Add `parsed_json jsonb` column to store the structured `ParseResult` - Both columns should default to `NULL` for backward compatibility with existing rows - Mirror these columns in the canonical schema file `apps/web/supabase/schemas/53-materials.sql` **`updateMaterial` function** (`apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/server/materials.service.ts`): - Add `updateMaterial(client, materialId, updates)` accepting partial updates for `parseStatus`, `content`, and `parsedJson` - Use `materialsTable(client).update().eq("id", materialId).select("*").single()` — consistent with the existing `createMaterial` pattern - Return the full updated row **Zod schema** (`apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts.ts`): - Add `MaterialParseStatusSchema` as `z.enum(["pending", "processing", "done", "error"])` - Add nullable `parseStatus` and `parsedJson` fields to `MaterialItemSchema` ```
### Phase 2: Processing Engine Implementation Replace the placeholder `processDeck()` with real parsing logic and wire it into the upload flow.
Task 1: Implement processDeck with Document Parsing Replace the stub implementation with real PPTX/PDF parsing using the existing `DocumentParserService`. - In `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/[id]/assemble/_lib/server/deck-processing.service.ts`: - Import `createDocumentParserService` and `SupportedFormat` from `@kit/mastra/rag` - Import `updateMaterial` from the materials service - Import `getLogger` from `@kit/shared/logger` for error tracking - Determine format fr ## Coding Rules # Coding Rules These rules document the actual code quality patterns used in the SlideHeroes codebase. ## TypeScript Patterns - Strict mode compliance: enable all strict TypeScript options - Use explicit local type aliases rather than inline complex types - Use `z.infer` for Zod-derived types - Use `instanceof Error` narrowing in catch blocks instead of `instanceof` checks on non-Error values - Never use `any` - use `unknown` with type guards when needed ## API Route Patterns - Use Next.js 15 style with `params` as Promise - Use named exports (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) for route handlers - Use `.catch(() => ({}))` for body parsing error handling - Use `NextResponse.json({ error })` response shape - Validate input at boundaries using Zod schemas ## Prisma Patterns - Import Prisma client from `@/lib/db` - Use conditional spread for PATCH updates (e.g., `...(data || {})`) - Use `.catch(() => null)` for not-found handling ## Import Conventions - Use `@/` alias for src imports (e.g., `@/components/Button`) - Import from specific files, avoid barrel exports - Separate client and server imports - never mix them from the same file ## Error Handling - Use tight try/catch around specific I/O operations only - Use `logger.error({ err }, '...')` for structured logging - Import logger from `@kit/shared/logger` - Handle errors gracefully using try/catch and appropriate error types - Use `isRedirectError` from `next/dist/client/components/redirect-error` for client-side redirect handling ## React Patterns - Encapsulate repeated blocks of code into reusable local components - Write small, composable, explicit, well-named components - Always use `react-hook-form` and `@kit/ui/form` for forms - Use client components only when needed for interactivity (use `use client` directive) - Add `data-testid` for E2E tests where appropriate - Prefer server-side data fetching using RSC - Display loading indicators where appropriate ## Next.js Patterns - Use `enhanceAction` for Server Actions from `@kit/next/actions` - Use `use server` in server action files - Export page components using the `withI18n` utility - Add well-written page metadata to pages - Redirect using `redirect` following a server action instead of using client-side router ## Data Fetching Patterns - Use async server components that call loader functions for initial data fetching - Use `enhanceAction` for create/update/delete operations with schema validation - Use `Promise.all()` for parallel data fetching in Server Components ## Linting and Formatting Before committing, always run: ```bash pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck ``` ## Component Organization - Route-specific components: `_components/` directories - Route utilities: `_lib/` for client, `_lib/server/` for server-side - Global components: Root-level directories ## Naming Conventions - Pages: `page.tsx` (Next.js convention) - Loaders: `{feature}-page.loader.ts` - Actions: `{feature}-server-actions.ts` - Schemas: `{feature}.schema.ts` - Components: `kebab-case.tsx`
label: neo-issue-2576
